TRANSCRIPT CONTEXT:
[157.080-434.930] interviewee: the army, the Polish Army. Since I was a graduate of the high school, I had the right to join the officer school. The Polish didn't like Jewish officers. And they, instead of taking them in, they said, oh, he is sick or something, and they released him. They would rather release him instead of keeping him in the officer school. But with me, it was different. It was in a small town. Everybody knew me. I was a very well known athlete in soccer and in tennis. So they couldn't refuse it. So

WORDS AT THE SMILE MOMENT:
they released him. They would rather release him instead
